 {"id":"bd-1gdn","title":"Add lipgloss individual border edge colors and Transform method","description":"Add per-edge border colors and Transform method to Lipgloss.

Scope:

- Implement Transform method for style manipulation consistent with Go (e.g., normalize or mutate styles).
- Add unit tests for per-edge colors and Transform behavior.
- Update docs if API changes are user-visible.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Per-edge border colors render correctly in multiple combinations.
- Transform method behaves as documented and has unit coverage.
- Conformance fixtures updated if needed to include per-edge color scenarios.","notes":"Files: crates/lipgloss/src/style.rs, border.rs, renderer.rs. Maintain backward compatibility where practical (but no shims).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:48.923880193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T18:15:01.451539959Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T18:15:01.450257984Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1i7e","title":"demo_showcase: CLI contract (modes, flags, help)","description":"Define the binary UX for launching the demo.

Proposed modes/flags (finalize based on UX concept):
- default: run interactive local TUI
- `--theme <name|auto>`
- `--theme-file <path>` (optional)
- `--seed <u64>` (stable demo data)
- `--no-animations`
- `--no-mouse`
- `--no-color` (force ASCII)
- `--no-alt-screen` (run without alt-screen; useful for debugging + demonstrating bubbletea println/printf)
- `--self-check` (headless CI-friendly render + exit)

Optional subcommands:
- `ssh --addr :2222 --host-key <path>` (wish integration; feature-gated)
- `export --html <path>` / `export --plain <path>` (optional: export snapshots)

Acceptance:
- `--help` is clean and self-explanatory.
- Flags map directly to `demo_showcase::Config` fields.
- Error messages are user-friendly (bad theme name, bad seed, missing file).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:30.810927082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:34:48.539935175Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:34:48.539870655Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1i7e","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:05.884573066Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i7e","depends_on_id":"bd-szmb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:30.830556320Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1isw","title":"huh: Fix FilePicker single-file selection edge case","description":"# Bug: FilePicker Cannot Select Single File

## Problem Statement
In the FilePicker navigation logic, the down-arrow condition prevents
selecting the only file when there's exactly one file:

```rust
// Lines 3660-3663
if self.selected_index < self.files.len().saturating_sub(1) {
    self.selected_index += 1;
    // ...
}
```

When files.len() == 1:
- saturating_sub(1) = 0
- condition: 0 < 0 = FALSE
- navigation does nothing

## Root Cause Analysis
The condition `< len - 1` is meant to prevent going past the last item,
but it also prevents selecting the first (and only) item when starting
from index 0.

Actually, the bug is in the logic design. The condition should allow
moving TO the last item, not just items BEFORE the last.

Wait, re-reading: if selected_index is 0 and len is 1:
- Can't move down because 0 < 0 is false
But the item at index 0 IS selectable - it's already selected by default.

The real issue might be different - let me trace the full logic.

## Actual Issue
The condition prevents moving DOWN when already at last item, which is correct.
But if the initial state has selected_index pointing elsewhere or if there's
a reset that doesn't account for single-item lists, selection could be broken.

Let me re-examine:
```rust
// When len=1, saturating_sub(1)=0
// selected_index starts at 0 (presumably)
// Down key: 0 < 0 is false, no change (correct - already at end)
// Up key: 0 > 0 is false, no change (correct - already at start)
```

This is actually correct for navigation. The bug might be elsewhere -
perhaps in initialization or the actual selection/confirm logic.

## Further Investigation Needed
1. Check initial selected_index value for single-file directories
2. Check if Enter/confirm works with single file
3. Check if the file is visually highlighted

## Potential Fix Areas
If init doesn't set selected_index correctly:
```rust
fn set_files(&mut self, files: Vec<PathBuf>) {
    self.files = files;
    self.selected_index = 0.min(self.files.len().saturating_sub(1));
}
```

If confirm doesn't work:
```rust
fn confirm(&self) -> Option<PathBuf> {
    self.files.get(self.selected_index).cloned()
}
```

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn filepicker_single_file() {
    let mut picker = FilePicker::new("/single-file-dir");
    // Simulate single file
    picker.files = vec![PathBuf::from("only_file.txt")];
    picker.selected_index = 0;
    
    // Should be able to select it
    let view = picker.view();
    assert!(view.contains("only_file.txt"));
    assert!(/* file is highlighted */);
    
    // Confirm should return the file
    let selected = picker.confirm();
    assert_eq!(selected, Some(PathBuf::from("only_file.txt")));
}

#[test]
fn filepicker_empty_directory() {
    let mut picker = FilePicker::new("/empty-dir");
    picker.files = vec![];
    
    // Should handle gracefully
    let view = picker.view();
    assert!(view.contains("No files") || view.is_empty() == false);
    
    let selected = picker.confirm();
    assert!(selected.is_none());
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single-file directories work correctly
- [ ] Empty directories handled gracefully
- [ ] Navigation works for 0, 1, and many files
- [ ] Selection/confirm works in all cases
- [ ] Tests added for edge cases","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:05.943176512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:05.967235547Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["edge-case","filepicker","huh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1isw","depends_on_id":"bd-2xzk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:05.967192347Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1knu","title":"Quality: headless self-check mode (CI-friendly)","description":"Add a non-interactive mode (e.g., `demo_showcase --self-check`) that validates the demo can boot and render headlessly.

Why:
- CI needs a fast "does it basically work" signal without a real TTY.
- Self-check also prevents subtle regressions in bootstrap/theming/rendering.

What self-check does:
- Construct the Config (deterministic defaults).
- Bootstrap the App model.
- Render a small set of views/pages (at a fixed size), for example:
  - Dashboard
  - Jobs
  - Docs
- Optionally run a tiny scripted navigation (no user input required).

Output:
- On success: exit 0.
- On failure:
  - exit non-zero
  - print a concise error + where artifacts were written
  - write a small artifact bundle (last rendered frame, config snapshot) under `target/demo_showcase_e2e/` or similar.

Constraints:
- Must work in `TERM=dumb` / no-tty environments.
- Must never hang (max-step/time guard).

Acceptance:
- Self-check runs quickly and deterministically in CI.
- Failures are actionable without rerunning locally.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:29.630984785Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:33:32.778490535Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1knu","depends_on_id":"bd-13np","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:35.814496203Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1knu","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:26.513764136Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1knu","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:29.649380944Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1knu","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:29.218612410Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1kx7","title":"E2E: extend CI script to run full scenario suite","description":"Extend the E2E runner script to run the full demo_showcase scenario suite (not just smoke), with great logging and CI-friendly controls.

Why:
- The demo_showcase is a huge integration surface; regressions will happen.
- A single, well-instrumented E2E entrypoint makes it easy to validate “everything still works” locally and in CI.

Requirements:
- Profiles:
  - `smoke` (fast): minimal navigation, no panics, basic rendering assertions.
  - `full` (slower): runs every scenario and validates key outputs/artifacts.
  - optional `nightly` (extra slow): stress/resizes, multiple seeds, async-enabled run.
- Scenario selection:
  - allow selecting a subset (e.g., `--scenario docs` or `--scenario wizard,logs`).
- Determinism:
  - accepts a seed (or chooses and prints one) and writes it into the artifact directory.
- Observability:
  - structured JSONL log output + per-step timing
  - on failure, print a clear summary including artifact path and last N steps
- Exit codes:
  - non-zero on failure; zero on success.

Acceptance:
- CI can choose which profile to run (PR vs nightly) without editing the script.
- Failures are actionable: the artifact directory contains enough info to reproduce.

Testing:
- The script itself is validated by running at least the smoke suite in CI.
- Full suite runs either on demand or on nightly schedule.

Notes:
- Keep this script the canonical entrypoint; avoid multiple half-overlapping scripts.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:27.825203081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:28.537686113Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","script","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-1g80","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:01:31.237245937Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3449","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:42.979497295Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3ecr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:01:29.940423979Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3nrc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:41.602626554Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3spt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:01:32.598620003Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-6wkl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:28.537657400Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-miqo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:44.326429546Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:27.846212242Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1m7x","title":"E2E scenario: ssh mode smoke (optional/manual)","description":"Optional E2E scenario for wish integration.

Constraints:
- This may be too heavy for default CI.

Plan:
- Generate/use a temp host key.
- Start `demo_showcase ssh` on localhost with an ephemeral port.
- Connect with an SSH client (or a minimal russh client) and ensure:
  - the server accepts a session
  - the UI renders at least one frame
  - a quit sequence cleanly closes the session

Logging:
- Capture server logs + client transcript to artifacts.

Acceptance:
- Provides high confidence that wish integration stays alive over time.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:31.040331959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:33.585303389Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","ssh","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1m7x","depends_on_id":"bd-32ba","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:33.585268063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m7x","depends_on_id":"bd-3xs0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:32.328836611Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m7x","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:31.067983923Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1o6y","title":"Implement huh Multiselect, Text, FilePicker, Note field types","description":"Implement missing Huh field types: Multiselect, FilePicker, and Note. (Textarea is tracked separately under bd-212m.4.1.)

MultiSelect and Note were already present. All field types have builder pattern, validation, keyboard handling, theme support, and view rendering.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1oe0","title":"demo_showcase core: optionally use bubbletea derive macro for one model","description":"Optional showcase: use `bubbletea-macros` derive for *one* small model/component inside demo_showcase.

Why:
- It demonstrates that the workspace’s proc-macro ergonomics actually work in a real app.
- It also gives users a concrete reference for when the derive is worth using.

Scope:
- Pick a small, isolated model (examples):
  - a tiny counter widget in Settings/About
  - a modal state machine (open/close) with a few fields
- Do NOT macro-derive the entire App (keep core architecture explicit and readable).

Acceptance:
- The derived model compiles cleanly and behaves correctly.
- The code includes a brief doc comment explaining why derive was used here.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:52.572059183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:27:58.105847511Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","macros"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1oe0","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:52.588543294Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1oe0","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:30.485956680Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1p61","title":"Unit tests: domain action API correctness","description":"Add comprehensive unit tests for the domain action API.

Coverage:
- start job -> job exists + correct initial state
- progress ticks update job correctly
- cancel job -> state transitions + logs/notifications
- retry failed job -> new attempt semantics
- deploy action -> deployment + job correlation

Logging:
- Tests should log action inputs + resulting state diffs for easy debugging.

Acceptance:
- The action API is covered with high confidence and runs fast.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:05.168723242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:24:21.174597921Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:24:21.174533320Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1p61","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:05.181377320Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1p61","depends_on_id":"bd-3pm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:19.061233110Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-33fe","title":"demo_showcase data: structured log stream + formatting","description":"Create the underlying structured log stream used by the demo (domain simulation + user actions).

Why:
- The Logs page should not be "just strings". We want structured fields so filtering/search is fast and correct.
- It proves the integration story: domain layer emits events, UI renders + filters them.

Deliverables:
- A `LogEntry` type (or similar) with fields like:
  - timestamp / monotonic tick
  - level
  - service/component
  - message
  - optional correlation ids (job id, deployment id)
- A log buffer/stream:
  - append-only collection with a bounded retention policy (optional)
  - API to push new entries from simulation/actions

Integration:
- The Logs viewer uses this stream and a formatter (`Logs: styled log formatting using charmed_log`) to render.
- Filtering operates on structured fields, not re-parsed rendered text.

Acceptance:
- Large log volumes remain responsive.
- Entries support correlation (wizard → job → logs) and filtering by level/service.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:53.990998123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:32:29.336843390Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:32:29.336748333Z","close_reason":"Implemented structured log stream with filtering. Enhanced LogEntry with tick, job_id, deployment_id correlation. Created LogStream with filtering by level/target/job/deployment/text/tick-range, count_by_level(), and bounded retention. Added 16 new tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33fe","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:54.007146912Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33fe","depends_on_id":"bd-2hwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:48.231233898Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33mv","title":"demo_showcase UX: define product concept + information architecture","description":"Pick a single cohesive "product" story and define the information architecture.

Recommendation (unless a better idea emerges): **Charmed Control Center** — an ops-style console for a fictional platform (services, deployments, jobs, logs, docs, settings).

Define:
- Primary navigation items (pages) and what each proves about the stack.
- Layout primitives (app chrome): header (status), sidebar (nav), main content, footer (hints).
- Core user journeys:
  - Navigate dashboards, drill into a service/job, view logs, open docs, run a wizard, browse files.

-{"id":"bd-35d2","title":"E2E: headless scripted runner (custom IO + recorded inputs)","description":"Implement an E2E runner that can drive the app without a real TTY.

Core approach:
- Run the app in custom I/O mode (bubbletea `with_input`/`with_output`) so it does not touch the real terminal.
- Feed deterministic input into the program:
  - Prefer a semantic step DSL (key presses, text input, resize, mouse click/scroll).
  - Optionally support raw byte streams to exercise the InputParser explicitly.
- Capture rendered frames (strings) so tests can assert on UI output.
- Terminate deterministically (send quit key / QuitMsg; enforce a max-step guard).

Time determinism:
- Avoid wall-clock sleeps.
- Advance simulation/animations by injecting tick messages and/or running a fixed "frame pump" loop.
- Ensure scenarios can run with animations disabled to reduce flakiness.

Assertions:
- Provide helpers for:
  - `assert_contains("Jobs")` / `assert_not_contains("\x1b[")` (no-color)
  - page header checks
  - optional direct AppState assertions (if the runner has access to the final model)

Integration:
- Must plug into the structured artifact logger (`E2E: structured test logging + artifact capture`).

Acceptance:
- Scenarios are readable scripts: "press j", "open docs", "search", "quit".
- Runner failures explain *where* and *why* (step id + last frame + config snapshot).","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:32.886754241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:08:14.968086813Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-13np","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:37.168687580Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:03.434318654Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:05.951709495Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:04.674114434Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:32.910218872Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:07.191517307Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-39hl","title":"Dashboard: uptime stopwatch + incident countdown widget","description":"Exercise bubbles time components in a realistic, high-polish dashboard context.

Why:
- `bubbles::stopwatch` and `bubbles::timer` are core "real app" primitives (uptime, ETAs, countdowns).
- Time widgets also stress the update loop (tick scheduling, formatting, focus/reduce-motion behavior).

Widgets to implement:
- Uptime (stopwatch):
  - displayed as `DD:HH:MM:SS` (or similar) with compact formatting
  - clearly indicates running/paused state (pause if unfocused is optional)
- Incident SLA / next maintenance window (timer countdown):
  - counts down to an upcoming event
  - visual emphasis as it nears zero (color + subtle animation)

Integration:
- Drive updates via bubbletea tick/every (no manual sleeps).
- Respect global toggles:
  - reduce-motion / `--no-animations` (no flashy pulsing)
  - focus/blur (optionally throttle when unfocused)

Acceptance:
- Widgets look polished and update smoothly.
- No excessive CPU usage (tick frequency is reasonable).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:24.377240967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:27:24.071286192Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","stopwatch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39hl","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:24.396412480Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39hl","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:26.892799063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39hl","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:28.950716964Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-39my","title":"demo_showcase data: async job runner demo (AsyncCmd)","description":"When the `bubbletea/async` feature is enabled, demonstrate a realistic async workload pipeline using `AsyncCmd` (non-blocking background work that produces messages).

Why:
- Async is a headline capability: it proves TUIs can do real I/O work without blocking input.
- The showcase should prove both *correctness* (no races/panics) and *ergonomics* (clean update logic).

What to demonstrate (in-app):
- I/O-like operations:
  - “fetch metrics”, “deploy service”, “load docs index”, “export logs”, etc.
  - each operation returns a message that updates state + triggers UI feedback
- Concurrency:
  - multiple AsyncCmds can run concurrently (batch)
  - results reconcile safely into the model
- Cancellation semantics (best-effort):
  - bubbletea AsyncCmd itself doesn’t inherently cancel tasks; demonstrate cancellation via:
    - per-job generation IDs / cancellation tokens
    - ignoring late results for canceled jobs
    - UI shows “canceled” and stops progress updates

Implementation notes:
- Feature-gate the async runner with `cfg(feature = "async")` and provide a sync fallback path so the demo still works without async.
- Prefer deterministic delays in test mode (e.g., fixed durations or seeded RNG) so E2E is stable.

Acceptance:
- Async mode feels like a real app doing background work while remaining responsive.
- No input blocking; no terminal corruption; no runaway task spawning.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - async pipeline updates state correctly (use tokio test + deterministic time where feasible)
  - cancellation token/generation logic ignores late results
  - batch/sequence composition behaves as expected
- E2E:
  - at least one scenario runs with async enabled (can be a separate CI job if needed) and asserts job progression + completion.

Notes:
- Keep the API surface small: most of the complexity should live in demo_showcase domain state, not in glue.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:52.895189964Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:44:26.567807730Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39my","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:52.911117280Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39my","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:53.253293494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39my","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:51.982805847Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-39te","title":"Unit tests: println/printf gating (only when no-alt-screen)","description":"Add unit tests ensuring the demo_showcase uses bubbletea `println/printf` intentionally and only in the right mode.

Why:
- `println/printf` is a powerful feature but can easily create messy output if used in alt-screen.
- We want a crisp, deterministic policy: only emit unmanaged lines when the user explicitly opts in.

Test coverage:
- When Config indicates alt-screen is enabled (default):
  - events that would normally log/notify do *not* produce `PrintLineMsg` commands.
- When Config indicates `--no-alt-screen`:
  - the same events do produce `bubbletea::println/printf` output (i.e., `PrintLineMsg`).

Implementation hint:
- Keep the policy in a small helper (e.g., `maybe_print_line(config, msg) -> Cmd`) so tests can execute the Cmd and assert on the returned Message type.

Acceptance:
- Tests are deterministic and do not require a real terminal.
- Failures clearly indicate which mode produced unexpected output.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:29.095304097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:29.120255253Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39te","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:29.120238122Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39te","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:29.110807003Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39te","depends_on_id":"bd-7iul","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:29.115944759Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3jg2","title":"Interactions: bracketed paste support in inputs/search","description":"Bubbletea delivers paste sequences as `KeyMsg` with `paste=true` (and/or via terminal paste events).

Why:
- Paste is a "real app" interaction: users paste queries, IDs, paths, and commit hashes.
- Terminals can deliver large paste payloads; naïve handling can lag or break inputs.

Ensure paste works sensibly in:
- Jobs query bar
- Logs query/filter input
- Docs search
- Wizard text fields (huh)
- Notes scratchpad (textarea)

Edge cases:
- Very large paste:
  - should not freeze the UI
  - inputs should apply paste in a bounded way (e.g., respect char limits)
- Multi-line paste:
  - for search inputs: collapse whitespace/newlines into spaces (or clearly defined behavior)
  - for textarea: preserve newlines

Acceptance:
- Pasting a multi-word query "just works" across inputs.
- No hangs or panics with large paste payloads.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:37.767936662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:18:16.372890345Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:18:16.372758850Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input","paste"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jg2","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:37.786648712Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3jg2","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:58.203351507Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ju1","title":"charmed_log: Thread Safety & Reliability Hardening","description":"# Epic: charmed_log Thread Safety & Reliability Hardening

## Background
The charmed_log crate provides structured logging with lipgloss styling. During deep review,
several critical issues were identified that can cause application crashes or silent data loss.

## Critical Issues Identified

### 1. Lock Poisoning on RwLock::unwrap() - CRITICAL
**Location**: Lines 427, 468, 475, 481, 488, 494, 500, 506, 512, 518, 528, 579, 597
**Problem**: Every read/write lock acquisition uses .unwrap() which panics if lock is poisoned.
Lock poisoning occurs when a thread panics while holding a lock.
**Impact**: A single panic in any formatting routine breaks ALL future logging in the application.
**Risk**: Production reliability - one panic → application crash cascade

### 2. Silent I/O Error Discarding - CRITICAL
**Location**: Line 598: `let _ = inner.writer.write_all(output.as_bytes());`
**Problem**: Write failures to stderr go completely unnoticed (disk full, permission denied, pipe closed).
**Impact**: Logs silently disappear without any notification.
**Risk**: Silent data loss in production - critical audit logs may vanish

### 3. Backtrace Overhead in Caller Reporting - HIGH
**Location**: Line 308: `let bt = Backtrace::new();`
**Problem**: Every log call with report_caller=true captures and parses full stack trace.
**Impact**: 100x+ slower than regular logging when caller reporting is enabled.
**Risk**: Severe performance degradation if accidentally enabled in production

### 4. Double Lock Pattern Race Window - MEDIUM
**Location**: Lines 579-598
**Problem**: log() acquires read lock, formats, drops, then acquires write lock.
Configuration could change between the two lock acquisitions.
**Impact**: Possible inconsistency in output formatting under concurrent configuration changes.

## Design Philosophy for Fixes
- Logging should be resilient - never crash the application
- Errors should be reported through a configurable error handler
- Performance-critical paths should have explicit opt-in for expensive features
- Thread safety should use recover-from-poisoning strategy for logging

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All lock acquisitions handle poisoning gracefully
- [ ] I/O errors are propagated to configurable error handler
- [ ] Backtrace overhead is documented with performance warning
- [ ] All changes have regression tests
- [ ] Zero clippy warnings","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:15:10.326116387Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:00:22.270057599Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:00:22.269990013Z","close_reason":"All child beads complete: bd-3lwk (error handler), bd-1bja (lock poisoning), bd-2go9 (caller overhead docs), bd-3lk7 (double-lock fix). charmed_log now has robust thread safety and reliability.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","critical","thread-safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ju1","depends_on_id":"bd-gyzr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:15:10.342713910Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3kcq","title":"demo_showcase: product concept + UX + visual spec","description":"Define the *product story* and UX blueprint for the demo_showcase app so the implementation is cohesive (not a grab-bag of components).

Deliverables (in-beads, not a separate doc):
- App concept (what it is pretending to be), core user journeys, navigation IA.
- Keybindings + mouse interactions philosophy (keyboard-first, mouse-supported).
- Visual system: spacing scale, typography/weight usage, borders, accent colors, status semantics.
- Accessibility/readability constraints: contrast, focus states, "NO_COLOR"/ASCII fallbacks.

Acceptance:
- All subsequent implementation issues can reference this epic for the intended "feel" and UX rules.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:02.355567902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:02.371452730Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kcq","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:02.371406974Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3kk5","title":"Docs: implement markdown viewer (glamour + viewport)","description":"Implement the Docs page viewer UI: beautiful Markdown rendering in a real app chrome.

Why:
- The Docs page is a flagship glamour showcase and doubles as onboarding.
- It must look great *and* behave like a real reader (fast, searchable, resizable).

Requirements:
- Render markdown with glamour using:
  - current theme
  - current content width
  - syntax/line-number toggles (if enabled)
- Display rendered output inside `bubbles::viewport`.
- Scrolling:
  - smooth line/page scrolling
  - clear scroll position indication (optional)
- Resize:
  - re-render on width changes (avoid re-render loops)

Performance considerations:
- Cache the last rendered output per doc + width + theme/toggles to avoid unnecessary work.

Acceptance:
- Markdown looks great and is readable; scrolling is smooth.
- Resizing produces correct reflow without broken borders.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:41.644067143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:30:03.289589115Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","glamour","viewport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kk5","depends_on_id":"bd-1y5q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:20.700839493Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kk5","depends_on_id":"bd-22vc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:41.663813227Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kk5","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:15.479047421Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kk5","depends_on_id":"bd-39bb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:23.368496686Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kk5","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:18.119084229Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3kk5","title":"Docs: implement markdown viewer (glamour + viewport)","description":"Implement the Docs page viewer UI: beautiful Markdown rendering in a real app chrome.

Why:
- The Docs page is a flagship glamour showcase and doubles as onboarding.
- It must look great *and* behave like a real reader (fast, searchable, resizable).

Requirements:
- Render markdown with glamour using:
  - current theme
  - current content width
  - syntax/line-number toggles (if enabled)
- Display rendered output inside `bubbles::viewport`.
- Scrolling:
  - smooth line/page scrolling
  - clear scroll position indication (optional)
- Resize:
  - re-render on width changes (avoid re-render loops)

Performance considerations:
- Cache the last rendered output per doc + width + theme/toggles to avoid unnecessary work.

 {"id":"bd-3kvw","title":"Quality: performance sanity pass","description":"Do a performance sanity pass on the showcase to ensure it stays smooth and doesn’t hide O(n^2) traps.

Why:
- A flagship demo must feel snappy; if it’s sluggish, the whole library looks bad.
- This also protects us from accidental performance regressions as features pile on.

What to check:
- Rendering hot paths:
  - avoid rebuilding huge strings when only a small viewport slice changed
  - avoid per-frame allocations in common paths
- Logs page:
  - thousands of lines should remain scrollable
  - filtering/search should not reformat everything per keypress
- Docs page:
  - avoid re-rendering markdown on unrelated keypresses
- Animations:
  - tick scheduling should be bounded and respect reduce-motion/unfocused state

Deliverables:
- A short write-up (in the issue) of findings + any fixes.
- Optional: lightweight instrumentation counters (render time, allocations) if easy.

Acceptance:
- No obvious performance cliffs in the major pages.
- The demo remains responsive on modest terminals.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:32.584483527Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:30:37.010151502Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kvw","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:32.603361544Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kvw","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:42.556770050Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kvw","depends_on_id":"bd-iwbw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:39.828524091Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kvw","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:37.203751874Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3lk7","title":"charmed_log: Fix double-lock race window in log() method","description":"# Task: Fix Double-Lock Race Window in log() Method

## Problem Statement
The log() method has a brief window where configuration could change between
acquiring the read lock (to format) and acquiring the write lock (to write):

\`\`\`rust
// Current implementation
pub fn log(&self, level: Level, msg: &str, keyvals: &[(&str, &str)]) {
    let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
    // ... format output using inner's settings ...
    drop(inner);  // Release read lock
    
    // GAP: Another thread could modify configuration here
    
    let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
    let _ = inner.writer.write_all(output.as_bytes());
}
\`\`\`

## Race Condition Scenario
1. Thread A calls log(), acquires read lock
2. Thread A reads formatter=JSON, starts formatting
3. Thread A drops read lock
4. Thread B calls set_formatter(Text), acquires write lock
5. Thread B changes formatter to Text, releases lock
6. Thread A acquires write lock
7. Thread A writes JSON-formatted output (but formatter is now Text)

## Impact Analysis
- **Low severity**: Configuration changes during logging are rare
- **Inconsistent output**: Single log entry could use mixed settings
- **Not a crash**: Just potentially confusing output

## Solution Design

### Option A: Hold Read Lock, Capture Writer Handle
\`\`\`rust
pub fn log(&self, level: Level, msg: &str, keyvals: &[(&str, &str)]) {
    // Single write lock for entire operation
    let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
    
    let output = self.format_output(&inner, level, msg, keyvals);
    
    if let Err(e) = inner.writer.write_all(output.as_bytes()) {
        self.handle_write_error(&mut inner, e);
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Option B: Copy-on-Read for Immutable Config
\`\`\`rust
struct LogConfig {
    formatter: Formatter,
    level: Level,
    // ... other immutable config
}

pub fn log(&self, level: Level, msg: &str, keyvals: &[(&str, &str)]) {
    let config = {
        let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
        inner.config.clone()  // Clone config snapshot
    };
    
    let output = self.format_with_config(&config, level, msg, keyvals);
    
    let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
    let _ = inner.writer.write_all(output.as_bytes());
}
\`\`\`

### Recommended: Option A
Simpler implementation, single lock acquisition, atomic operation.
Performance cost is minimal (write lock slightly slower than read).

## Implementation Steps
1. Refactor log() to use single write lock
2. Move formatting inside the lock scope
3. Handle error within the same lock scope
4. Ensure no deadlock risk (CallerInfo::capture happens outside lock)
5. Add concurrent test to verify atomicity
6. Benchmark to verify no performance regression

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
\`\`\`rust
#[test]
fn log_configuration_is_atomic() {
    use std::sync::{Arc, Barrier};
    use std::thread;
    
    let logger = Logger::new();
    logger.set_formatter(Formatter::Json);
    
    let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(3));
    let output = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::new()));
    
    // Thread 1: Change formatter repeatedly
    let logger1 = logger.clone();
    let b1 = barrier.clone();
    let t1 = thread::spawn(move || {
        b1.wait();
        for _ in 0..100 {
            logger1.set_formatter(Formatter::Text);
            logger1.set_formatter(Formatter::Json);
        }
    });
    
    // Thread 2: Log messages
    let logger2 = logger.clone();
    let b2 = barrier.clone();
    let output2 = output.clone();
    let t2 = thread::spawn(move || {
        b2.wait();
        for i in 0..100 {
            logger2.info(&format!("msg{i}"), &[]);
        }
    });
    
    barrier.wait();
    t1.join().unwrap();
    t2.join().unwrap();
    
    // Each log line should be valid JSON or valid Text, never mixed
    let output = output.lock().unwrap();
    for line in String::from_utf8_lossy(&output).lines() {
        assert!(
            serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(line).is_ok() ||
            !line.starts_with('{'),
            "Malformed line: {line}"
        );
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Performance Benchmark
\`\`\`rust
#[bench]
fn bench_log_single_threaded(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let logger = Logger::new();
    b.iter(|| {
        logger.info("benchmark message", &[("key", "value")]);
    });
}

#[bench]
fn bench_log_multi_threaded(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let logger = Logger::new();
    let logger = Arc::new(logger);
    
    b.iter(|| {
        let handles: Vec<_> = (0..4).map(|_| {
            let l = logger.clone();
            thread::spawn(move || {
                for _ in 0..100 {
                    l.info("benchmark", &[]);
                }
            })
        }).collect();
        for h in handles {
            h.join().unwrap();
        }
    });
}
\`\`\`

### E2E Test Script
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
# Test: charmed_log_race_condition.sh

set -e
echo "=== charmed_log Race Condition Tests ==="

# Run with thread sanitizer if available
if command -v rustup &> /dev/null; then
    RUSTFLAGS="-Z sanitizer=thread" cargo test --package charmed_log log_configuration_is_atomic -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee /tmp/charmed_log_race.log
else
    cargo test --package charmed_log log_configuration_is_atomic -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee /tmp/charmed_log_race.log
fi

echo "Test output saved to /tmp/charmed_log_race.log"
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] log() uses single lock acquisition
- [ ] Configuration reads and writes are atomic
- [ ] No performance regression (benchmarked)
- [ ] Thread sanitizer passes (no data races)
- [ ] Unit test demonstrates atomic behavior
- [ ] E2E test script added","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:23:09.231404292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:00:06.160040947Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:00:06.159966068Z","close_reason":"Fixed double-lock race window by using single write lock for entire log() operation. Consolidated format+write into atomic operation, added associated function versions of format methods to avoid &self borrow conflicts.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","concurrency","race-condition"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lk7","depends_on_id":"bd-1bja","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:23:09.254472794Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lk7","depends_on_id":"bd-3ju1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:23:09.249157523Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3lwk","title":"charmed_log: Add configurable error handler for I/O failures","description":"# Bug: Silent I/O Error Discarding

## Problem Statement
Line 598 silently discards all I/O errors:
```rust
let _ = inner.writer.write_all(output.as_bytes());
```

When writing to stderr fails (disk full, permission denied, pipe closed, etc.),
the error is completely ignored. Critical log messages vanish without any indication.

## Impact Analysis
- **Audit Logs**: Compliance-critical logs may silently disappear
- **Debugging**: Error messages lost when most needed (during failures)
- **Monitoring**: Log-based alerting becomes unreliable
- **Forensics**: Missing logs create gaps in incident investigation

## Scenarios Where This Fails
1. Disk full on log directory
2. Log file permissions changed
3. Pipe to log collector closed unexpectedly
4. stdout/stderr redirected to closed file descriptor
5. Docker container stdout buffer overflow

## Solution Design

### Option A: Configurable Error Handler (RECOMMENDED)
Add an optional error handler callback:

```rust
pub struct Logger {
    inner: Arc<RwLock<LoggerInner>>,
}

struct LoggerInner {
    // ... existing fields ...
    error_handler: Option<Box<dyn Fn(std::io::Error) + Send + Sync>>,
}

impl Logger {
    pub fn with_error_handler<F>(self, handler: F) -> Self
    where
        F: Fn(std::io::Error) + Send + Sync + 'static
    {
        let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
        inner.error_handler = Some(Box::new(handler));
        drop(inner);
        self
    }
}

// In log():
if let Err(e) = inner.writer.write_all(output.as_bytes()) {
    if let Some(ref handler) = inner.error_handler {
        handler(e);
    }
    // Optionally: attempt fallback to stderr if primary fails
}
```

### Option B: Return Result
Not recommended - changes the entire API contract and makes logging
awkward (callers don't want to handle errors from logging).

### Default Behavior
If no error handler is configured:
- First failure: Print warning to stderr (if available)
- Subsequent failures: Silent (to avoid infinite loops)

## Implementation Steps
1. Add error_handler field to LoggerInner
2. Add with_error_handler() builder method
3. Modify log() to call handler on I/O failure
4. Add fallback-to-stderr option
5. Add tests for error handling scenarios
6. Document the error handling behavior

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn error_handler_called_on_io_failure() {
    let error_captured = Arc::new(Mutex::new(None));
    let error_clone = error_captured.clone();
    
    let logger = Logger::new()
        .with_writer(FailingWriter)
        .with_error_handler(move |e| {
            *error_clone.lock().unwrap() = Some(e.to_string());
        });
    
    logger.info("test", &[]);
    
    assert!(error_captured.lock().unwrap().is_some());
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Error handler callback mechanism implemented
- [ ] Default behavior documented (silent after first warning)
- [ ] Tests for various I/O failure scenarios
- [ ] Example in docs showing error handler usage
- [ ] No breaking changes to existing API","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:15:57.520589539Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:23:21.251959454Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:22:55.633760095Z","close_reason":"Implemented configurable error handler for I/O failures. Added ErrorHandler type, with_error_handler() builder method, proper error handling in log() with default warn-once behavior, and 5 new tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","critical","reliability","silent-failure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lwk","depends_on_id":"bd-1bja","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:23:21.251928716Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lwk","depends_on_id":"bd-3ju1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:15:57.542307390Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
